extra.reasoning: The property requires that setting pallor=true (all else fixed) never lowers acuity. pallor appears in exactly one rule, r5 (priority 100, the dominating priority), whose output is self_care — the LOWEST acuity. So activating r5 by setting pallor true drives acuity down. Concrete counterexample (pallor=true witness): severe_headache=palpitations=focal_weakness=fever=pallor=true; back_pain=rash=dizziness=false. With pallor=FALSE: r5 cannot fire (pallor missing); matching rules are r2 (palpitations->emergent, p3), r4 (fever->emergent, p3), r6 (severe_headache->urgent, p2), r7 (focal_weakness->urgent, p2); highest priority 3 wins => acuity = emergent. With pallor=TRUE (only pallor flipped): r5's full conjunction is satisfied; at priority 100 it dominates all p3/p2 rules => acuity := self_care. Acuity drops from emergent to self_care, which is lower in the enum order, violating monotonicity.
